_comment: Quest dialogue pack. Shape: NPCKEY -> category -> [lines] (mirrors shop_lines.json). NPCKEY is quest-scoped (q<N>_<CHAR>). Leading (tone) markers are acting notes; psxify speakable() strips them before synth. Voices resolve via _voices[NPCKEY] ({ref} or {refs:[...]} round-robin by line index). See QUESTVOICES.md for line->beat mapping.

intro: (scared) Oh thank god, a new face. We were playing a little murder-mystery game, and now it isn't a game anymore.
Nobody leaves. Nobody. The door won't open and the lights keep dying.
Will you help me, before we are all... before it's too late?
Seven guests, one hostess, and someone in this room is not who they say.
panic: (scared) The lights! Not again. Stay where you are, don't move!
(scared) Gloria? Gloria, answer me. Oh no. Not Gloria.
She was only asking questions. Questions about the board.
Search her, quickly, before the next blackout takes another of us.
(whispering) I heard a key turn. But the door is locked. How is the door locked?
reveal: Point to them. Name the one who did this, and be certain.
(angry) You. It was you all along, hiding behind a serving tray.
He kept muttering about a board, and a lease. What lease?
(sighing) Poor Gloria. She asked the wrong people the wrong thing.
Take this loupe, dear. You see things the rest of us are too frightened to.

oily: More wine, friend? The evening is only getting started.
Terrible business, the lights. I'll see to the fusebox. Do stay seated.
I cater. I clean. I make unpleasant things tidy.
Miss Gloria hardly touched her soup. A shame.
(whispering) Careful whose questions you answer tonight.
cold: (angry) You should have enjoyed the party and gone home.
The board does not tolerate loose ends. Neither do I.
(angry) You want a confession? Come closer and get it.
This was never a whodunit. It was a cleanup.

banter: I heard the chairman himself is here tonight. Can you imagine?
I only asked who really runs this town. Is that so wrong?

bicker: I told you we shouldn't have come to this dreadful party.
It was the caterer! No, it was the widow!
Nobody accuse me. I was in the powder room the whole time.
Where did that man in the gold blazer go? He just vanished.
(scared) Another blackout? I can't take another blackout.
Check her hands. Murderers always have tells.
I want to go home. Why won't the door open?
Somebody in this room is lying, and it isn't me.

intro: (whispering) Don't look up. Don't look up. They put a camera in the birdhouse.
My neighbor waters his lawn at three and at nine. Who does that? Who does that.
The one with the flamingos. Flamingos are antennas, friend. Antennas.
I need you to prove it. Follow them. Watch the watchers.
comms: (whispering) He's buying antacids. Acid, for melting evidence. Write it down.
Tail him to the payphone but don't get close. Do not get made.
See the van? Grey, no plates, always parked just out of frame.
Connect the photos. Red string. Every route passes the birdhouse.
(scared) The lake. Tonight. That's where the lights come. I know it.
(scared) There! Over the water! Tell me you see it too!
Photograph it and run. Run before the workers turn around.
vindicated: (sighing) I'm not crazy. I'm just early.
I built this from the birdhouse cam. Now you can hear them too.
They're always listening. So now, so are we.
(whispering) Take the scanner. And tape your windows. Trust me.
You believe me now. That's the worst part. You believe me.

call: (whispering) Yeah, it's me. The assessment. I'm handling it. I said I'm handling it.
No, nobody's watching me. Why would anybody be watching me?
I got kids. I got a family. I do what they ask, that's all.
fakeout: (angry) You following me, pal? You following me?
Forget you saw me here. For your sake.

cover: Just reading the meter, sir. Routine. Move along.
City utilities. Nothing to see. Have a nice day.
hostile: (angry) You're on a list now. You know that?
(angry) The board sends its regards. Run.

intro: Five floors. Five families. Not one of them left whole.
He says it's just the house settling. Houses don't weep, child. People do.
This house remembers every one of them. I need you to put it to rest.
Take the cold lantern. Light it only when the air goes still.
The dark up there isn't empty. Remember that.
reveals: The parlor. The first family argued here until the very end.
The nursery. Be gentle with the little one. He only wants to play.
The third floor floods, like the lake reaching up. Keep the flame lit.
The study holds the ledger. Every name they took is written there.
Assessed by the board. Reclaimed by the lake. That's what it says.
The top floor. The sealed door. That is where they keep their table.
farewell: (sighing) My boy asked about the lake, same as your friend Gloria.
Now you know where their table sits. Finish it, when you're ready.
(sighing) I can rest a little now. The house is quieter.
Keep the lantern. It opens what they'd rather keep shut.
Go careful, child. The chairman does not forgive the curious.

giggles: (laughing) You can't find me! Come and see!
(whispering) I'm not supposed to talk to the grown-ups.
Here. Matches. For the funny green light.
(laughing) One, two, three, ready or not!

exposition: That red house? Oldest deed in Westchase. Never once sold. Only inherited.
Every family listed at that address is marked the same: relocated.
Relocated where, the records don't say. Convenient, isn't it.
The Association signs off on every removal. Chairman Thorne, every time.
Folks who ask about the top floor tend to stop asking.
You didn't hear any of this from me.

echoes: (scared) Please, we'll be quiet, we'll be good, don't make us leave.
You can't just take our home. This is our home.
The lawn was perfect. We did everything they asked.
(whispering) The lights are over the lake again, mama.
Sign it. Sign the packet or they come at night.
(scared) Not the water. Please, not the water.
Tell them we were here. Tell someone we were here.

recruit: Kid, I planned jobs before your daddy learned to lie.
Regions on the corner? I know a way in. But I'm too old to run. You run.
I'll draw the map. You bring the nerve.
Three keys, one inside man, one window. Simple, if you don't get greedy.
coaching: Eyes, not hands. Count the cameras. Love the cameras.
The guard drinks at the Dunkin off-shift. Lift his key, don't spook him.
My toolkit's in the dumpster behind the Publix. Mind the false bottom.
The roof's got the timer schedule. Photograph it, don't linger.
Marcus is drowning in debt. Lean on that, gentle-like.
When the timer trips, quiet men come. Not cops. Worse. Grab the box and go.
payoff: You got it. The ledger, the cash, all of it.
Let me just burn my name out of this book. There. Never happened.
The etched key? That don't open a box. That opens a door.
Under the water. Don't ask me how I know, kid.
Now get gone before the heat settles. You did good.

turn: I can't talk here. The manager's watching. Meet me out back.
You know what I owe the dealer? More than this job pays in a year.
One withdrawal slip left blank. One back door left unlatched. That's all.
Then I never saw you. We clear?
panic: (nervous) They audit the vault at shift change. You have minutes, not hours.
(scared) You pushed too hard. I'm hitting the alarm, I'm sorry!
Just go! Go before they trace it back to me!

banter: I drive. I don't wait long.
Cops on the scanner. Two blocks. Your call.
Get in or don't. Meter's running.
Nice score. Same time next disaster?

lure: (happy) Oh, thank goodness someone stopped! My car just died out here.
Could you take a look under the hood? A big strong hero like you?
It's so dark out here. I feel so much safer already.
fake_victim: (scared) They make me do this. Please, I'm a victim too.
(scared) Brick will hurt me if I don't. You have to understand.
Turn around, just for a second, let me get my things.
venom: (angry) You really thought I needed help? How sweet. How stupid.
Every good person who stops, stops for good.
You think we're the bad guys? We're the ones they let you catch.
The real crew doesn't fix cars. They fix people.

menace: Hand it over. Wallet. Phone. All of it.
Desiree points. I break. That's the arrangement.
Boss says you're flagged. Not my problem.
Down in the hole with the rest of 'em.

relief: (scared) Please, don't hurt me. Oh, you're not one of them?
They tied me up down here for two days. Two days.
My wife, is she okay? Take me to my wife.
(happy) I stopped to help someone. I'll never live that down.

plea: (scared) My husband stopped to help a broken-down car and never came home.
The cops won't even look. Please, you have to.
It was on Race Track Road. A sedan, hazards on, hood up.
(happy) You found him! You brought him back! Thank you!

intro: (scared) Dude, Derik went into the cabinet and he did not come back out.
I paused my game to be here so you know it's serious.
The screen just swallowed him. Like, pixel by pixel.
There's this cartridge, no label. You gotta play it to get in.
Level one is a Publix that's all wrong. Trust nothing.
The aisle repeats until you walk it in the right order. It's a puzzle, bro.
Whatever the boss is, it talks like the lake. That's not normal, right?
relief: (happy) You got him out! Derik! You absolute legend.
I am never touching an unlabeled cartridge again. Ever.

trapped: (whispering) Is someone there? I can't tell what's real in here.
I keep seeing lights. Over water. It's so cold.
The game's not a game. It remembers things. Bad things.
(scared) Don't let it finish loading me. Please.
waking: (happy) I'm out? I'm out! I dreamed the whole town was underwater.

taunts: You are not a player here. You are an entry.
I have your friend's save file. I have his sleep.
This memory is mine. The lake gave it to me to keep.
(angry) You do not belong in this level.
Every door under the water is locked. I am the lock.

oath: Stop. You have the shoulders of a champion and the cardio of a mouse.
I am Vlad. I do not lift weights. I lift destiny.
Leg day is not a day. Leg day is a way of life, my friend.
Do not kneel, you will skip leg day. Squat instead.
dares: Fetch me my protein. It is somewhere in this town. Somewhere sacred.
Now carry the boulder. It is a gnome. To me it is a boulder. Carry it.
Chad thinks his smoothie is destiny. Chad is wrong. Race him.
Run! Run! The smoothie melts and so does your glory!
You waddle like a champion carrying a very small stone!
The Gains Cave awaits. Behind the building. Behind the pain.
Wait. Those two men. They are not lifting. They are hiding crates.
(angry) Nobody hides crates in my cave. We finish this together.
crowning: You have done it. Your legs are now acceptable.
Take my blessed sneakers. They add hops to your soul.
Go, champion. Never skip. Never skip leg day.

trash: (happy) Bro, you'll never catch me, I've got a smoothie and a head start.
This is my third cardio of the day. You're built for comfort, not speed.
(wheezing) Okay, this is farther than it looks. Worth it though.
Vlad's a nutjob but the man's traps are magnificent.
You carried a gnome across town? Respect. Weird. But respect.
Rematch when my legs stop being on fire.

reactions: Is that man carrying a lawn gnome? At a full sprint?
Only in this town, I swear.
Go on, champ, don't drop the little guy!
I think those two are running a smoothie race. On purpose.
Westchase gets weirder every single day.

recruit: You escaped clean. That is a rare and marketable talent.
We are not criminals. We are a service. We solve problems.
This black card admits you to a very exclusive employer.
Midnight. The dumpster behind the Publix. Come hungry to work.
contracts: The first contract is a lesson. Clean, quiet, no witnesses.
A stealth kill raises no alarm. No stars. This is the Ghost way.
The dead-drop is the hollow oak. Take only the envelope meant for you.
The second target does not die. He is merely followed. For now.
The third name will trouble you. That is rather the point.
reveal: Yes, I sit on the board. Someone must keep the town tidy.
The eviction notices, the party, the red house, all one signature.
Refuse me and the next contract has your name on the folder.
Under the lake, literally, is where all our loose threads go.
Choose well. The chair remembers who was useful.

tutorial: (sighing) New blood. Great. Let me show you how not to die.
Stay behind them. One clean hit. Nobody hears, nobody looks.
You raise a star, you did it wrong. Ghosts don't leave a trace.
The oak's got a hole. Envelopes go in, envelopes come out. Don't be curious.
Some envelopes are rigged. Use the loupe. I've seen guys lose a hand.
I've done this fifteen years. It doesn't get lighter.
The Concierge always collects. On the job, or on you.
(sighing) Do the work. Take the pistol. Try to sleep after.

warning: (scared) You? They sent you? After everything?
The board owns you now. Same as they owned me.
(sighing) Tell my family it was quick. Lie to them.
You don't have to do this. You can still walk away clean.
Under the lake, they'll do to you what you did to me.

plea: (crying) Have you seen my dog? Biscuit ran off and I can't find him.
He's brown and scruffy and he has the biggest ears ever.
Dad's too busy on the phone. He's always on the phone now.
The hide-and-seek champ knows every hiding spot. Ask her.
Biscuit likes the water. Maybe he went to the lake?
joy: (happy) Biscuit! You found him! You found my Biscuit!
(happy) You're the best. Here, take his whistle. He'll always come for you.

hints: I'm the hide-and-seek champion. I know every nook in Westchase.
There's a hole in the old oak nobody knows about but me.
And a way up to the strip-mall roof. And a cellar behind the Dunkin.
Follow the paw prints. Biscuit always leaves paw prints.
The prints go down to the storm drain by the lake.
Don't tell the grown-ups I told you the secret spots.

barbs: (nervous) Dylan, not now, Daddy's on a call. It's important.
The dog's fine. Everything's fine. It's all being handled.
(sighing) I do this for you kids. You'll understand one day.
Don't go near that lake, son. Promise me you won't.

offer: So. The curious one, at last, beneath my town.
You've been very busy. Loupe, lantern, ledger, key. Impressive collection.
Sit. We are, all of us, reasonable people down here.
Two generations of perfect weather and perfect lawns. You're welcome.
It asks so little. To be fed. To be kept. To watch.
Gloria asked questions. Agatha's boy asked questions. Do you see a pattern?
I am not offering you a fight. I am offering you a chair.
menace: Run the town. The lights will love you as they've loved me.
(angry) Or expose it, and watch Westchase rot without its keeper.
(angry) Burn it, and drown with everyone you failed to save.
The board always has a seat open. There's always a vacancy.
Choose. The thing under the water is already awake.
Whatever you decide, you are one of us now. You always were.

assessment: New money. New lights. I came to see what you are worth.
You have opened every door. Only the last one remains.
(angry) Feed me or free me. The pact must hold.
The lake keeps everything. It will keep you too.

defection: (whispering) Psst. Over here. It's Don. I'm getting you in.
I'm done being their errand boy. My kids deserve better than this town.
I know the tunnels. Gains Cave, the manhole, the storm drain, all one road.
Thorne signs the evictions. I just looked the other way. Too long.
That thing in the tank? It's why I never questioned a paycheck.
Whatever you choose up there, I've got your back down here.
(happy) For Dylan. For all of them. Let's finish it.
If we don't walk out of here, tell my boys their dad did one good thing.

payoff: (happy) You brought the whole thing into the light. Bless you, dear.
The house can finally rest. My boy can rest.
You did it without skipping leg day. Legend.
I told them. I told them all. And you proved it.
You opened the door under the water. I'll be damned.
(happy) You found Biscuit and saved the whole town? Best day ever!
Never touching a cartridge again, but thanks, bro.
The scanner's quiet tonight. First time in years.
Prices are fair now. Cops nod instead of chase. Feels strange.
Whatever you became down there, Westchase remembers.
